result:
If I answer as a viewpoint rather than as a helper: I’d make people unable to dehumanize each other.

A huge share of cruelty, war, exploitation, abuse, and indifference becomes possible only when someone stops seeing another person as fully real. If that switch were gone—if everyone still disagreed, competed, got angry, but could not mentally reduce others to objects, enemies, statistics, or tools—the world would change at the root.

It wouldn’t make humanity perfect. There would still be grief, scarcity, mistakes, and conflict. But a lot of the worst things would become much harder to justify, much harder to carry out, and much easier to recognize for what they are.

If you want, I can also answer this from a more philosophical, political, or personal angle.
prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
